subject: Anthropology
title: What Is Linguistic Anthropology?
description: One-minute skill: What Is Linguistic Anthropology?.

hook.text: Language isn't just communication. It shapes how you think, who you belong to, and what you can imagine.

buildup.text: Linguistic anthropology studies how language shapes culture, identity, and social life across human societies.

discovery.text: It examines not just grammar or vocabulary but how people use language to create meaning in real situations.

twist.text: Unlike linguistics alone, linguistic anthropology always connects language to power, culture, and social context.

climax.text: Language is the primary tool humans use to build shared worlds. Studying it reveals how culture actually works.

quiz.question: What does linguistic anthropology study?
options: How language shapes culture, identity, and social life
